one_time_description: Advance on $570,000,000 land claim settlement with Government of Canada received prior to March 31, 2024; net other income after trust deposits and per capita distributions was $4,110,756
economic_notes: Nation operates gas station, wood products business, and hotel (Cumberland Crossing Inn Limited Partnership 51%). Subsequent to year-end settled land claim for $570,000,000 and agricultural claim for ~$118,802,000. $500,000,000 deposited to Community Development Trust, $30,800,000 to CHCN Children's Trust, $70,760,000 per capita distributions paid, $30,025,000 debt repaid, $22,400,000 legal and consulting fees. Took $40,000,000 loan ahead of settlement for per capita distribution (repaid at statement date).
notes: Large one-time item: $50,000,000 advance on land claim settlement received before March 31, 2024 (full settlement $570M agreed subsequent to year-end). Auditor's report includes Emphasis of Matter for subsequent event re settlements. $13,500,000 CHCN Temporary Trust promissory note classified as current and repaid subsequent to year-end. Nation not in compliance with financial covenants on $3,629,753 of loans, reclassified as current. CHCN Temporary Trust excluded from consolidation as controlled by Trustee. Two GBEs (SRBC and CHSRLP) on modified equity method. Consolidated entities include Saskatchewan River Community Development Corporation, Cumberland House Investment Corporation, Cumberland Crossing Inn LP (51%), and certain hotel building assets in Cumberland House Sask River LP. Province is Saskatchewan.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
